description: A museum creates a large touchscreen display showing the human digestive system. Visitors tap on each organ to see a 3D animation of its function and read a short description written in everyday language.

explanations.purpose: The goal is to teach visitors how the digestive system works — a classic educational purpose.
explanations.format: The digestive system follows a sequential path from mouth to intestine, making it a linear format.
explanations.complexity: 3D animations and per-organ descriptions provide rich detail, making this a detailed infographic.
explanations.audience: Everyday language and a museum setting target the general public, not specialists.
explanations.domain: Human anatomy bridges both science (biology) and health — either answer is correct.

description: A consulting firm builds a dashboard for a hospital CEO comparing patient wait times, staff utilization, and readmission rates across five departments, with quarterly trend lines and a drill-down into each metric.

explanations.purpose: The dashboard exists to reveal patterns in operational data — an analytical purpose.
explanations.format: Comparing metrics across five departments side by side makes this a comparative format.
explanations.complexity: Multiple metrics, trend lines, and drill-down capability make this highly detailed.
explanations.audience: Built specifically for a CEO to support decisions — a stakeholder audience.
explanations.domain: Hospital operations and patient care place this in the health domain.

description: A startup creates a sleek one-page visual highlighting their project management tool's key features, customer logos, a pricing comparison table, and a prominent 'Start Your Free Trial' button.

explanations.purpose: Feature highlights, customer logos, and a free trial button are classic promotional elements.
explanations.format: The pricing comparison table aligns attributes side by side — a comparative format.
explanations.complexity: A clean one-page layout with curated highlights is minimalist by design.
explanations.audience: Project management tools target working professionals, not the general public.
explanations.domain: A software tool for project management falls in the technology domain.

description: A history professor creates an interactive timeline of the Civil Rights Movement from 1954 to 1968. Students click each event to read primary source excerpts and see photographs from the era.

explanations.purpose: The timeline teaches historical events with primary sources — clearly educational.
explanations.format: A chronological timeline arranges events in a sequential path — a linear format.
explanations.complexity: Primary source excerpts and photographs at each node add rich detail.
explanations.audience: Students in a history class represent a broad educational audience — general public.
explanations.domain: The Civil Rights Movement is a history topic.

description: An environmental advocacy group creates a dramatic visual showing rising sea levels alongside photos of flooding in coastal cities, ending with the headline 'The cost of inaction' and a petition sign-up form.

explanations.purpose: Emotional imagery, a charged headline, and a petition form are persuasive techniques designed to motivate action.
explanations.format: The narrative builds sequentially from data to photos to a call-to-action — a linear progression.
explanations.complexity: The message is streamlined for impact with a few bold elements rather than deep data — minimalist.
explanations.audience: A public petition targets everyday citizens — general public.
explanations.domain: Sea level rise and climate science place this in the science domain.

description: A data science team at a retail company visualizes two years of sales data in a radial chart, with each spoke representing a product category and ring layers showing seasonal quarters, so analysts can spot which categories peak in which seasons.

explanations.purpose: The visualization exists to help analysts discover seasonal sales patterns — an analytical purpose.
explanations.format: Spokes radiating from a center with ring layers is a radial format.
explanations.complexity: Two years of multi-category, multi-quarter data with layered rings is detailed.
explanations.audience: Data analysts at a company are professional users with domain expertise.
explanations.domain: Retail sales analysis is a business domain activity.
